tasks.title: Phase 1: Foundation & Utilities
tasks.description: Create Python infrastructure alongside shell scripts
tasks.details: Set up Python utilities module and requirements to support the migration. This phase creates the foundation without breaking existing shell scripts.
tasks.testStrategy: Import common.py in Python REPL, verify all functions work correctly

subtasks.title: Create Python utilities module (common.py)
subtasks.description: Create testing-infra/common.py with Python equivalents of common.sh functions
subtasks.details:
Functions to implement:
- setup_project_root()
- setup_logging()
- log() and log_and_echo()
- display_balances()
- get_aptos_address()
- fund_and_verify_account()

Keep common.sh for backward compatibility during migration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Import common.py in Python REPL, test each function individually

subtasks.title: Add Python requirements file
subtasks.details:
Add required packages:
- requests (for HTTP calls)
- subprocess (builtin)
- json (builtin)
- pathlib (builtin)

Add Python virtual env to .gitignore
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'pip install -r requirements.txt' and verify all dependencies install successfully

tasks.title: Phase 2: Teardown Scripts (Low Risk)
tasks.description: Convert smallest, safest scripts - pure cleanup operations
tasks.details: Start with teardown scripts as they are the simplest and safest to convert. These scripts only perform cleanup operations and have minimal complexity.
tasks.testStrategy: Run Python version after setting up chains, verify chains stop cleanly and all cleanup completes

subtasks.title: Convert stop-dual-chains.sh to Python (31 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert stop-dual-chains.sh (31 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Docker compose down for both chains
- Cleanup Aptos CLI profiles (alice-chain1, bob-chain1, alice-chain2, bob-chain2, intent-account-chain1, intent-account-chain2)
- Logging via common.py

Keep .sh version with deprecation notice during migration.
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Set up dual chains using shell script
2. Run Python stop_dual_chains.py
3. Verify both chains stopped
4. Verify all profiles deleted

subtasks.title: Convert stop-evm-chain.sh to Python (62 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert stop-evm-chain.sh (62 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Stop Hardhat node
- Docker cleanup for EVM chain
- Logging via common.py
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Set up EVM chain using shell script
2. Run Python stop_evm_chain.py
3. Verify EVM chain stopped
4. Verify all Docker containers cleaned up

tasks.title: Phase 3: Simple Setup Scripts (Medium Risk)
tasks.description: Convert straightforward setup scripts without complex orchestration
tasks.details: Convert setup scripts that have straightforward logic and don't call other scripts. These are more complex than teardown but still relatively isolated.
tasks.testStrategy: For each script: run setup, verify services are healthy, run corresponding teardown

subtasks.title: Convert deploy-vault.sh to Python (83 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert deploy-vault.sh (83 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Deploy vault contract
- Verify deployment
- Logging via common.py
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Setup EVM chain
2. Run deploy_vault.py
3. Verify vault deployed successfully
4. Check contract address is valid

subtasks.title: Convert setup-dual-chains.sh to Python (99 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert setup-dual-chains.sh (99 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Docker compose setup for both chains
- Health checks
- Profile creation
- Logging via common.py
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run setup_dual_chains.py
2. Check both chains are healthy (curl endpoints)
3. Verify profiles created
4. Run stop_dual_chains.py for cleanup

subtasks.title: Convert setup-evm-chain.sh to Python (151 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert setup-evm-chain.sh (151 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Start Hardhat node
- Docker setup for EVM chain
- RPC health checks
- Logging via common.py
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run setup_evm_chain.py
2. Verify Hardhat RPC responding (curl http://127.0.0.1:8545)
3. Check eth_blockNumber works
4. Run stop_evm_chain.py for cleanup

tasks.title: Phase 4: Complex Setup & Orchestration (Higher Risk)
tasks.description: Convert scripts that call other scripts or have complex logic
tasks.details: These scripts orchestrate multiple operations and may call other scripts. Require careful testing to ensure all dependencies work correctly.
tasks.testStrategy: Full integration testing with all dependent scripts, verify end-to-end flows work correctly

subtasks.title: Convert setup-and-test-alice-bob.sh to Python (177 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert setup-and-test-alice-bob.sh (177 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Orchestrates setup and testing
- Alice/Bob account funding
- Balance verification
- Transaction testing
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run setup_and_test_alice_bob.py
2. Verify Alice and Bob accounts funded
3. Check balance transfers work
4. Verify final balances are correct

subtasks.title: Convert setup-and-deploy.sh to Python (112 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert setup-and-deploy.sh (112 lines) to Python.

Features:

- Contract deployment
- Profile initialization
- Verification
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run setup_and_deploy.py
2. Verify chains running
3. Check contracts deployed
4. Verify profiles exist with correct addresses

subtasks.title: Convert setup-and-deploy-evm.sh to Python (198 lines)

subtasks.title: Convert setup-dual-chains-and-test-alice-bob.sh to Python (202 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert setup-dual-chains-and-test-alice-bob.sh (202 lines) to Python.

Features:

subtasks.title: Convert release-evm-escrow.sh to Python (214 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert release-evm-escrow.sh (214 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Escrow release logic
- Event monitoring
- Balance verification
- State transitions
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Setup escrow scenario
2. Run release_evm_escrow.py
3. Verify escrow released
4. Check balances updated correctly
5. Verify events emitted

tasks.title: Phase 5: Test Runners & Critical Scripts (Highest Risk)
tasks.description: Convert complex orchestration scripts that call multiple other scripts
tasks.details: Most complex scripts that orchestrate entire test flows. These are critical to the testing infrastructure and require comprehensive testing.
tasks.testStrategy: Full E2E test suite must pass for each conversion. Run multiple times to ensure reliability.

subtasks.title: Convert run-tests.sh (APT) to Python (110 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert run-tests.sh (110 lines - APT) to Python.

Features:
- Orchestrates full E2E test flow
- Calls setup, deploy, and test scripts
- Updates verifier configs
- Runs Rust integration tests
- Cleanup
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run run_tests.py
2. Verify all E2E tests pass
3. Check verifier config updated correctly
4. Verify cleanup completes
5. Run multiple times for reliability

subtasks.title: Convert run-tests.sh (EVM) to Python (254 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert run-tests.sh (254 lines - EVM) to Python.

Features:
- EVM E2E test orchestration
- Multiple script coordination
- Contract deployment verification
- Test execution
- Cleanup
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run run_tests.py
2. Verify all EVM E2E tests pass
3. Check contracts deployed correctly
4. Verify cleanup completes
5. Run multiple times for reliability

subtasks.title: Convert submit-cross-chain-intent.sh to Python (367 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert submit-cross-chain-intent.sh (367 lines) to Python.

Features:
- Cross-chain intent submission
- Multi-chain coordination
- Event monitoring
- State verification
- Complex error handling
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Setup dual chains
2. Run submit_cross_chain_intent.py
3. Verify intent submitted on chain 1
4. Check intent received on chain 2
5. Verify state transitions correct
6. Check events emitted properly

subtasks.title: Convert submit-cross-chain-intent-evm.sh to Python (341 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert submit-cross-chain-intent-evm.sh (341 lines) to Python.

- Event monitoring

- State verification
subtasks.testStrategy:

3. Verify intent submitted on EVM

subtasks.title: Convert run-cross-chain-verifier.sh to Python (507 lines)
subtasks.details:
Convert run-cross-chain-verifier.sh (507 lines) to Python.

This is the most complex script with extensive logic. Consider breaking into smaller functions/modules.

Features:
- Full verifier flow orchestration
- Multi-chain coordination
- Event monitoring
- State verification
- Complex error handling
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Setup all chains
2. Run run_cross_chain_verifier.py
3. Verify verifier processes intents correctly
4. Check all state transitions
5. Verify cross-chain consistency
6. Run complete E2E flow multiple times

tasks.title: Phase 6: Cleanup & Documentation (Low Risk)
tasks.description: Finalize migration, update docs, remove shell scripts
tasks.details: Complete the migration by updating documentation, adding backward compatibility if needed, and cleaning up old shell scripts.
tasks.testStrategy: Run full test suite with Python-only setup, verify all documentation is accurate

subtasks.title: Update documentation
subtasks.description: Update README files to reference Python scripts
subtasks.details:
Updates needed:
- README files in testing-infra/
- Python setup instructions
- Requirements and dependencies
- Examples of running Python scripts
- Migration notes
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Review all documentation
2. Follow setup instructions from scratch
3. Verify examples work
4. Check links and references

subtasks.title: Add shell script wrappers OR deprecate shell scripts
subtasks.details:
Two options:
A) Create thin .sh wrappers that call Python scripts for backward compatibility
B) Deprecate .sh files entirely and update all references

Discuss with team which approach to take.
subtasks.testStrategy:
If wrappers: verify .sh files correctly call Python versions
If deprecation: verify no broken references remain

subtasks.title: Remove common.sh, finalize Python-only setup
subtasks.description: Complete migration by removing shell utilities
subtasks.details:
Final steps:
- Remove common.sh
- Ensure all scripts use common.py
- Remove any remaining shell dependencies
- Update .gitignore if needed
- Final verification
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Remove common.sh
2. Run full test suite with Python-only setup
3. Verify no shell dependencies remain
4. Check all scripts work correctly
5. Run complete E2E tests
